feed.title: Combined summary - Quantum Attack Game Theory

entry.title: Combined summary - Quantum Attack Game Theory
entry.summary: The discussions surrounding BIP-361 highlight a common misconception regarding the impact of quantum computing on market dynamics. Contrary to popular belief, the introduction of a quantum computer into the market is likely to cause more than just temporary volatility. This complexity stems from various underlying factors that are not immediately obvious but could have profound long-term effects on market stability and security protocols. Further exploration into this topic can be found in Jameson Lopp's detailed analysis, which attempts to catalog potential outcomes of a quantum attack using game theory principles. His insights are available on his blog, which provides a more comprehensive understanding of the situation at [Jameson Lopp's Blog](https://blog.lopp.net/quantum-attack-game-theory/). Additionally, there are inquiries about the mathematical foundations related to blockchain reorganizations (reorgs). Questions have been raised regarding the standard methodologies for calculating certain formulas integral to understanding blockchain dynamics. An example includes how opportunity costs are computed when a blockchain participant chooses to pursue a failed reorg attempt. Typically, pursuing a failed reorg involves missing out on block rewards, which represents a significant opportunity cost if the reorg is unsuccessful. However, these costs are mitigated if the reorg attempt is successful, highlighting the complex risk-reward calculations miners need to consider in their strategies. This blend of theoretical and practical considerations forms a crucial part of ongoing discussions in blockchain technology and quantum computing impacts, emphasizing the need for a nuanced understanding of both technological advancements and their potential repercussions.
